claim.observation: Counting account-management event IDs in the parsed Security.evtx (base-dc) gives: 4720 account-created=0, 4722 enabled=0, 4726 deleted=0, 4728 added-to-global-group=0, 4732 added-to-local-group=0, 4756 added-to-universal-group=0, 4741 computer-account-created=0, 4724 password-reset=1, 4738 account-changed=1. The single 4724 record (0x00001274) and single 4738 record (0x00001282) both target the domain account tyler.oslund and are both timestamped Sep 06 2018 01:37:23 UTC on base-dc.shieldbase.lan. In the 4724 record the subject-name string is rsydow-a; in the 4738 record the subject-name string is ANONYMOUS LOGON.
claim.interpretation: Within this Security log's retained window there is no record of any new domain account being created (4720=0) and no record of any security-group membership being added (4728/4732/4756=0), nor any computer account creation (4741=0). The only account-management actions recorded are one password reset (4724) and one account modification (4738), both against the pre-existing account tyler.oslund at the same instant (2018-09-06 01:37:23 UTC). The 4724 record's subject fields name rsydow-a as the account that performed the reset. A domain password reset is a privileged operation; this record does not indicate whether it was authorized. The practical bearing on the 'unauthorized accounts' lead: the retained Security log holds no evidence of new or newly-group-privileged accounts being created during its coverage, so the on-disk profiles for other accounts were not accompanied by account-creation events inside this window.

evidence_pointer.locator: inode 103887; 4724+4738 records at 2018-09-06 01:37:23 UTC; target tyler.oslund (SID ...-1180)
evidence_pointer.tool: grep/awk over evtxexport text of Security.evtx
evidence_pointer.command: evtxexport -f text /tmp/sec.evtx ; grep -c per account-mgmt EID + awk RS extract of 0x1274 and 0x1282
